Atomic Concept: Understanding Rational Numbers
Key Skills: Define rational numbers; Convert fractions to decimals and vice-versa

Atomic Concept: Understanding Irrational Numbers
Key Skills: Define irrational numbers (e.g., √2, π); Recognize non-repeating, non-terminating decimals

Atomic Concept: Classifying Real Numbers (Rational vs. Irrational)
Key Skills: Categorize numbers as rational or irrational; Place on a number line

Atomic Concept: Understanding and Applying Properties of Rational Exponents
Key Skills: Define a^(m/n) = nth_root(a^m); Apply integer exponent properties to rational exponents

Atomic Concept: Simplifying Expressions with Rational Exponents
Key Skills: Use exponent rules (product, quotient, power rules) with fractional exponents

Atomic Concept: Simplifying Radical Expressions (Review & Extension)
Key Skills: Factor out perfect squares/cubes; Simplify radicals with variables

Atomic Concept: Adding and Subtracting Radical Expressions
Key Skills: Combine like radicals; Simplify before combining

Atomic Concept: Multiplying Radical Expressions
Key Skills: Apply distributive property; Use FOIL for binomials; Simplify products

Atomic Concept: Dividing Radical Expressions (Rationalizing the Denominator - Monomial)
Key Skills: Eliminate radicals from the denominator; Simplify quotients

Atomic Concept: Dividing Radical Expressions (Rationalizing the Denominator - Conjugates)
Key Skills: Use conjugates to rationalize binomial denominators

Atomic Concept: Solving Equations with Rational Exponents
Key Skills: Isolate term with rational exponent; Raise both sides to reciprocal power

Atomic Concept: Solving Radical Equations (with one radical)
Key Skills: Isolate the radical; Square/cube both sides

Atomic Concept: Solving Radical Equations (with multiple radicals or other terms)
Key Skills: Isolate one radical at a time; Square/cube both sides; Check for extraneous solutions

Atomic Concept: Understanding the Imaginary Unit *i* and its properties
Key Skills: Define *i* as √-1; Understand powers of *i* (e.g., i², i³, i⁴)

Atomic Concept: Simplifying Square Roots of Negative Numbers
Key Skills: Express √-b as i√b for b > 0; Simplify radical expressions with negative radicands

Atomic Concept: Understanding Complex Numbers and their Standard Form (a + bi)
Key Skills: Identify the real part and the imaginary part; Represent complex numbers in the form a + bi

Atomic Concept: Adding and Subtracting Complex Numbers
Key Skills: Combine real parts with real parts and imaginary parts with imaginary parts

Atomic Concept: Multiplying Complex Numbers
Key Skills: Apply the distributive property; Use i² = -1; Multiply binomials containing complex numbers

Atomic Concept: Understanding Complex Conjugates
Key Skills: Define the complex conjugate of a + bi as a - bi; Understand their role in division

Atomic Concept: Dividing Complex Numbers
Key Skills: Rationalize the denominator by multiplying by the complex conjugate

Atomic Concept: Solving Quadratic Equations with Complex Solutions (using Square Roots)
Key Skills: Apply the square root property to solve equations of the form x² = -c

Atomic Concept: Solving Quadratic Equations with Complex Solutions (using Quadratic Formula)
Key Skills: Use the quadratic formula when the discriminant is negative to find complex solutions

Atomic Concept: Interpreting Parts of an Expression (Terms, Factors, Coefficients, Exponents)
Key Skills: Identify components of expressions; Understand their meaning in context

Atomic Concept: Interpreting Complicated Expressions by Viewing Parts as a Single Entity
Key Skills: Recognize binomials or complex terms as single units (e.g., (x²-1) as a difference of squares)

Atomic Concept: Factoring Quadratic Expressions to Reveal the Zeros of the Function
Key Skills: Factor quadratic expressions to find x-intercepts or roots

Atomic Concept: Completing the Square in a Quadratic Expression to Reveal Vertex Form
Key Skills: Rewrite quadratic expressions to identify maximum or minimum values

Atomic Concept: Using Properties of Exponents to Rewrite Radical Expressions
Key Skills: Apply rules for integer and rational exponents to simplify and rewrite expressions involving radicals

Atomic Concept: Rewriting Rational Expressions in Different Forms
Key Skills: Factor and simplify rational expressions; Perform operations to rewrite forms

Atomic Concept: Choosing and Using Equivalent Forms of Expressions to Solve Problems
Key Skills: Select the most appropriate form (factored, standard, vertex) to answer questions

Atomic Concept: Analyzing Expressions in Context (e.g., projectile motion, financial models)
Key Skills: Interpret coefficients, terms, and factors in real-world scenarios

Atomic Concept: Review: Adding and Subtracting Polynomials
Key Skills: Combine like terms of two or more polynomials

Atomic Concept: Review: Multiplying Polynomials (Monomial by Polynomial, Binomial by Binomial)
Key Skills: Use distributive property; Apply FOIL method; Expand expressions

Atomic Concept: Understanding Closure Property of Polynomials (Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication)
Key Skills: Determine if the result of an operation on polynomials is always a polynomial

Atomic Concept: Understanding Polynomials as Analogous to Integers
Key Skills: Recognize structural similarities between polynomial arithmetic and integer arithmetic

Atomic Concept: Performing Polynomial Long Division
Key Skills: Divide polynomials using long division algorithm

Atomic Concept: Performing Synthetic Division of Polynomials
Key Skills: Use synthetic division for division by linear factors (x-k)

Atomic Concept: Review: Simplifying Rational Expressions
Key Skills: Factor numerator and denominator; Cancel common factors

Atomic Concept: Multiplying Rational Expressions
Key Skills: Factor expressions; Multiply numerators and denominators; Simplify products

Atomic Concept: Dividing Rational Expressions
Key Skills: Multiply by the reciprocal; Factor and simplify quotients

Atomic Concept: Finding the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of Polynomials
Key Skills: Determine the LCM of polynomial expressions to find common denominators

Atomic Concept: Adding and Subtracting Rational Expressions with Like Denominators
Key Skills: Combine numerators over common denominator; Simplify

Atomic Concept: Adding and Subtracting Rational Expressions with Unlike Denominators
Key Skills: Find common denominator; Rewrite expressions; Combine and simplify

Atomic Concept: Understanding Rational Expressions as Analogous to Rational Numbers
Key Skills: Recognize structural similarities between rational expression arithmetic and rational number arithmetic

Atomic Concept: Creating Equations and Inequalities in One Variable from Context
Key Skills: Translate verbal descriptions into linear or quadratic equations/inequalities with one unknown

Atomic Concept: Creating Equations in Two or More Variables to Represent Relationships
Key Skills: Define variables; Write equations that represent the relationship between two or more quantities

Atomic Concept: Creating Inequalities in Two Variables to Represent Constraints
Key Skills: Model real-world situations with linear or quadratic inequalities in two variables

Atomic Concept: Representing Constraints Using Equations or Inequalities (Contextual Problems)
Key Skills: Formulate a system of equations or inequalities to describe a situation

Atomic Concept: Rearranging Formulas to Highlight a Quantity of Interest (Literal Equations)
Key Skills: Isolate a specific variable in a formula or equation that has multiple variables

Atomic Concept: Creating Quadratic Equations from Graphs, Tables, or Verbal Descriptions
Key Skills: Identify key features (vertex, intercepts) to write the equation; Use given points to determine parameters

Atomic Concept: Creating Square Root Equations from Graphs, Tables, or Verbal Descriptions
Key Skills: Determine parameters to write equations for square root functions

Atomic Concept: Creating Inverse Variation Equations from Graphs, Tables, or Verbal Descriptions
Key Skills: Identify inverse relationship; Write equations for inverse variation functions

Atomic Concept: Solving Equations Graphically (Finding Intersections)
Key Skills: Use graphs to find approximate or exact solutions to equations

Atomic Concept: Understanding the Process of Solving Equations as a Logical Progression
Key Skills: Justify each step in solving equations; Explain the derivation of solutions

Atomic Concept: Solving Quadratic Equations by Factoring, Taking Square Roots, and Completing the Square
Key Skills: Choose and apply appropriate method to find all real and complex solutions

Atomic Concept: Solving Quadratic Equations using the Quadratic Formula (Real and Complex Solutions)
Key Skills: Apply the formula; Simplify solutions, including those with imaginary components

Atomic Concept: Solving Radical Equations (Focus on extraneous solutions)
Key Skills: Isolate radical; Square both sides; Check all solutions to avoid extraneous ones

Atomic Concept: Solving Rational Equations (Focus on extraneous solutions and domain restrictions)
Key Skills: Find common denominators; Clear denominators; Check for extraneous solutions due to domain restrictions

Atomic Concept: Solving Simple Exponential Equations (Common Base Method)
Key Skills: Rewrite terms with a common base; Equate exponents and solve

Atomic Concept: Solving Systems of Linear and Quadratic Equations (Algebraically)
Key Skills: Use substitution or elimination methods to find intersection points

Atomic Concept: Solving Systems of Linear and Quadratic Equations (Graphically)
Key Skills: Graph both equations; Identify and interpret points of intersection

Atomic Concept: Solving Quadratic Inequalities by Graphing
Key Skills: Graph the related parabola; Identify regions that satisfy the inequality; Write solution set

Atomic Concept: Solving Quadratic Inequalities Algebraically
Key Skills: Find critical points; Test intervals on a number line; Express solution set

Atomic Concept: Solving Systems of Inequalities (Linear and Quadratic) by Graphing
Key Skills: Graph each inequality; Shade the feasible region; Identify solution set

Atomic Concept: Understanding Function Notation and Evaluating Functions
Key Skills: Use function notation (f(x)); Substitute input values to find output values

Atomic Concept: Identifying Domain and Range of Functions (from graphs, equations, tables)
Key Skills: Determine input values and output values for quadratic, square root, and inverse variation functions

Atomic Concept: Relating the Domain of a Function to its Graph and Context
Key Skills: Identify reasonable domain restrictions based on the real-world scenario or graph

Atomic Concept: Calculating and Interpreting Average Rate of Change of a Function
Key Skills: Calculate average rate of change from tables or graphs; Interpret in context

Atomic Concept: Analyzing Key Features of Quadratic Functions from Graphs/Tables
Key Skills: Identify intercepts, intervals of increasing/decreasing, positive/negative, max/min, end behavior, symmetry

Atomic Concept: Analyzing Key Features of Square Root Functions from Graphs/Tables
Key Skills: Identify domain/range, endpoints, intervals of increasing/decreasing

Atomic Concept: Analyzing Key Features of Inverse Variation Functions from Graphs/Tables
Key Skills: Identify asymptotes, intervals of increasing/decreasing, domain/range

Atomic Concept: Graphing Quadratic Functions Given an Expression, Table, or Verbal Description
Key Skills: Create graphs of parabolas from different representations

Atomic Concept: Graphing Square Root Functions Given an Expression, Table, or Verbal Description
Key Skills: Create graphs of square root functions from different representations

Atomic Concept: Graphing Inverse Variation Functions Given an Expression, Table, or Verbal Description
Key Skills: Create graphs of hyperbolas from different representations

Atomic Concept: Comparing Properties of Two Functions Represented in Different Ways
Key Skills: Compare rates of change, intercepts, max/min, domain/range for functions represented graphically, tabularly, or algebraically

Atomic Concept: Writing a Function that Describes a Relationship between Two Quantities
Key Skills: Model real-world situations with equations in two variables

Atomic Concept: Combining Functions Using Addition and Subtraction
Key Skills: Perform (f+g)(x) and (f-g)(x) for given functions

Atomic Concept: Combining Functions Using Multiplication and Division
Key Skills: Perform (f • g)(x) and (f/g)(x) for given functions

Atomic Concept: Understanding Inverse Functions (Concept and Notation)
Key Skills: Define inverse functions; Understand that they "undo" each other; Use f⁻¹(x) notation

Atomic Concept: Finding the Inverse of a Function Algebraically
Key Skills: Swap x and y and solve for y to find the inverse equation

Atomic Concept: Graphing Inverse Functions (Reflection over y=x)
Key Skills: Graph a function and its inverse; Identify symmetry across the line y=x

Atomic Concept: Restricting Domain to Ensure an Inverse Function Exists
Key Skills: Identify domains for which a function (e.g., quadratic) has an inverse

Atomic Concept: Constructing Functions to Model Linear Relationships from Context
Key Skills: Write linear equations from tables, graphs, or verbal descriptions to model situations

Atomic Concept: Constructing Functions to Model Quadratic Relationships from Context
Key Skills: Write quadratic equations from tables, graphs, or verbal descriptions to model situations

Atomic Concept: Constructing Functions to Model Square Root Relationships from Context
Key Skills: Write square root equations from tables, graphs, or verbal descriptions to model situations

Atomic Concept: Constructing Functions to Model Inverse Variation Relationships from Context
Key Skills: Identify inverse relationship; Write equations for inverse variation functions

Atomic Concept: Determining an Explicit Expression or Recursive Process from Context
Key Skills: Translate real-world scenarios into explicit formulas (f(n) = ...) or recursive rules (f(n) = f(n-1) + ...)

Atomic Concept: Determining Steps for Calculation from a Context (Order of Operations)
Key Skills: Break down complex problems into a sequence of mathematical operations

Atomic Concept: Understanding Rigid Motions (Isometries)
Key Skills: Define rigid motion; Identify transformations that preserve distance and angle measure

Atomic Concept: Understanding Translations (Vector and Coordinate Rules)
Key Skills: Define translation; Represent translations using vectors or coordinate rules

Atomic Concept: Performing and Graphing Translations
Key Skills: Translate figures on the coordinate plane

Atomic Concept: Understanding Reflections Across Axes and Lines
Key Skills: Define reflection; Identify lines of reflection (x-axis, y-axis, y=x, y=-x)

Atomic Concept: Performing and Graphing Reflections
Key Skills: Reflect figures across lines on the coordinate plane

Atomic Concept: Understanding Rotations About the Origin (90°, 180°, 270°)
Key Skills: Define rotation; Understand angle and direction of rotation

Atomic Concept: Performing and Graphing Rotations About the Origin
Key Skills: Rotate figures on the coordinate plane using rules for specific angles

Atomic Concept: Understanding Compositions of Rigid Motions
Key Skills: Perform a sequence of two or more rigid transformations

Atomic Concept: Identifying Congruent Figures Based on Rigid Motions
Key Skills: Determine if two figures are congruent by identifying a sequence of rigid motions

Atomic Concept: Proving Triangle Congruence Criteria (SSS, SAS) using Rigid Motions
Key Skills: Understand how SSS and SAS follow from the definition of congruence based on rigid motions

Atomic Concept: Proving Triangle Congruence Criteria (ASA, AAS, HL) using Rigid Motions
Key Skills: Understand how ASA, AAS, and HL follow from the definition of congruence based on rigid motions

Atomic Concept: Using Congruence Criteria to Prove Triangle Congruence
Key Skills: Apply SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, HL to prove triangles congruent in two-column or paragraph proofs

Atomic Concept: Using CPCTC (Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are Congruent) in Proofs
Key Skills: Deduce additional congruent parts once triangles are proven congruent

Atomic Concept: Proving Theorems about Lines and Angles (Perpendicular Bisector, Angle Bisector)
Key Skills: Prove basic geometric theorems using deductive reasoning and postulates

Atomic Concept: Proving Theorems about Parallelograms
Key Skills: Prove properties of parallelograms (e.g., opposite sides/angles are congruent, diagonals bisect each other)

Atomic Concept: Understanding Dilations (Center, Scale Factor)
Key Skills: Define dilation; Determine coordinates of dilated figures

Atomic Concept: Understanding and Performing Similarity Transformations
Key Skills: Identify transformations that preserve shape but not size; Perform sequences involving dilations

Atomic Concept: Determining if Two Figures are Similar (Identifying Similarity Transformations)
Key Skills: Identify a sequence of transformations to show similarity; Understand proportional side lengths and congruent angles

Atomic Concept: Proving Triangles Similar using AA (Angle-Angle) Similarity
Key Skills: Apply AA criterion to prove triangle similarity; Identify corresponding angles

Atomic Concept: Proving Triangles Similar using SSS (Side-Side-Side) Similarity
Key Skills: Apply SSS criterion; Check proportionality of all three pairs of sides

Atomic Concept: Proving Triangles Similar using SAS (Side-Angle-Side) Similarity
Key Skills: Apply SAS criterion; Check proportionality of two sides and included angle congruence

Atomic Concept: Using Similarity to Find Missing Side Lengths in Triangles
Key Skills: Set up and solve proportions from similar triangles

Atomic Concept: Applying Similarity for Indirect Measurement and Problem Solving
Key Skills: Use similar triangles to find inaccessible lengths or heights in real-world contexts

Atomic Concept: Understanding and Applying the Geometric Mean Theorems in Right Triangles
Key Skills: Apply altitude and leg rules to find missing lengths in right triangles

Atomic Concept: Proving the Pythagorean Theorem using Triangle Similarity
Key Skills: Demonstrate the proof of the Pythagorean Theorem using properties of similar right triangles

Atomic Concept: Understanding Special Right Triangles (45-45-90)
Key Skills: Apply side ratios to find missing lengths in 45-45-90 triangles

Atomic Concept: Understanding Special Right Triangles (30-60-90)
Key Skills: Apply side ratios to find missing lengths in 30-60-90 triangles

Atomic Concept: Introduction to Trigonometric Ratios (Sine, Cosine, Tangent) in Right Triangles
Key Skills: Define SOH CAH TOA; Identify opposite, adjacent, hypotenuse

Atomic Concept: Understanding the Relationship Between Sine and Cosine of Complementary Angles
Key Skills: Recognize that sin(θ) = cos(90°-θ)

Atomic Concept: Using Trigonometric Ratios to Find Missing Side Lengths in Right Triangles
Key Skills: Set up and solve trigonometric equations to find unknown sides

Atomic Concept: Using Inverse Trigonometric Ratios to Find Missing Angle Measures in Right Triangles
Key Skills: Apply sin⁻¹, cos⁻¹, tan⁻¹ to find unknown angles

Atomic Concept: Solving Right Triangles (Finding all missing sides and angles)
Key Skills: Combine Pythagorean Theorem, trig ratios, and angle sum to solve entire triangles

Atomic Concept: Applying Angles of Elevation and Depression in Real-World Contexts
Key Skills: Solve problems involving angle of elevation/depression scenarios

Atomic Concept: Understanding Basic Circle Terminology
Key Skills: Define radius, diameter, chord, secant, tangent, arc, center, circumference

Atomic Concept: Understanding and Applying Properties of Tangent Lines to Circles
Key Skills: Prove that the tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency; Use properties to solve problems

Atomic Concept: Understanding Central Angles and Arc Measure
Key Skills: Define central angle; Relate central angle measure to arc measure (minor, major, semicircle)

Atomic Concept: Understanding Inscribed Angles and Their Properties
Key Skills: Define inscribed angle; Relate inscribed angle measure to intercepted arc measure

Atomic Concept: Understanding Angles Formed by Tangents and Chords
Key Skills: Relate the measure of an angle formed by a tangent and a chord to the intercepted arc

Atomic Concept: Understanding Angles Formed by Intersecting Chords (Inside the Circle)
Key Skills: Relate the measure of an angle formed by two chords intersecting inside a circle to the intercepted arcs

Atomic Concept: Understanding Angles Formed by Secants and/or Tangents (Outside the Circle)
Key Skills: Relate the measure of an angle formed by two secants, two tangents, or a secant and a tangent intersecting outside a circle to the intercepted arcs

Atomic Concept: Understanding Segment Lengths Formed by Two Chords
Key Skills: Apply the intersecting chords theorem to find unknown segment lengths

Atomic Concept: Understanding Segment Lengths Formed by Secants and/or Tangents
Key Skills: Apply theorems about segments formed by secants and tangents intersecting outside a circle

Atomic Concept: Calculating Arc Length
Key Skills: Use the formula for arc length in degrees or radians

Atomic Concept: Calculating Area of Sectors of Circles
Key Skills: Use the formula for the area of a sector in degrees or radians

Atomic Concept: Writing the Equation of a Circle (Standard Form)
Key Skills: Derive the equation of a circle; Write equation given center and radius

Atomic Concept: Graphing Circles from Standard Form
Key Skills: Identify center and radius from equation; Graph circles on the coordinate plane

Atomic Concept: Completing the Square to find the Equation of a Circle
Key Skills: Convert the general form of a circle's equation to standard form

Atomic Concept: Understanding Types of Statistical Studies (Surveys, Experiments, Observational)
Key Skills: Differentiate between study types; Identify advantages and disadvantages

Atomic Concept: Understanding Random Sampling and Random Assignment
Key Skills: Explain the purpose of random selection in sampling; Understand random assignment in experiments

Atomic Concept: Analyzing and Interpreting Data Distributions (Shape, Center, Spread)
Key Skills: Describe the overall pattern of a distribution; Identify outliers

Atomic Concept: Choosing Appropriate Measures of Center and Variability
Key Skills: Select mean/median and standard deviation/IQR based on distribution shape

Atomic Concept: Comparing Data Distributions Visually (Box Plots, Histograms)
Key Skills: Compare shape, center, and spread of two or more data sets presented graphically

Atomic Concept: Interpreting Two-Way Frequency Tables
Key Skills: Read and interpret data from two-way tables; Find marginal and joint frequencies

Atomic Concept: Calculating and Interpreting Conditional Probabilities from Two-Way Tables
Key Skills: Compute conditional probabilities; Understand independence based on conditional probabilities

Atomic Concept: Understanding Correlation and Causation
Key Skills: Differentiate between correlation and causation; Recognize confounding variables

Atomic Concept: Evaluating Statistical Claims and Data-Based Conclusions
Key Skills: Critically evaluate the validity of conclusions drawn from data; Identify potential biases

Atomic Concept: Review: Basic Probability (Theoretical vs. Experimental) and Sample Space
Key Skills: Define probability; Identify sample space and events; Calculate simple probabilities

Atomic Concept: Understanding Events: Independent vs. Dependent
Key Skills: Differentiate between independent and dependent events

Atomic Concept: Understanding Events: Mutually Exclusive vs. Inclusive
Key Skills: Differentiate between mutually exclusive and inclusive events; Use Venn diagrams

Atomic Concept: Understanding and Calculating Conditional Probability
Key Skills: Define conditional probability; Calculate P(A|B)

Atomic Concept: Using Two-Way Frequency Tables to Calculate Conditional Probability
Key Skills: Extract data from tables to compute conditional probabilities

Atomic Concept: Recognizing and Explaining Independence in Context (using conditional probability)
Key Skills: Determine if events are independent based on P(A|B) = P(A)

Atomic Concept: Applying the Multiplication Rule for Probability (Independent Events)
Key Skills: Calculate P(A and B) for independent events

Atomic Concept: Applying the Multiplication Rule for Probability (Dependent Events)
Key Skills: Calculate P(A and B) for dependent events using P(A and B) = P(A) • P(B|A)

Atomic Concept: Applying the Addition Rule for Probability (Mutually Exclusive Events)
Key Skills: Calculate P(A or B) for mutually exclusive events (P(A)+P(B))

Atomic Concept: Applying the Addition Rule for Probability (Inclusive Events)
Key Skills: Calculate P(A or B) for inclusive events (P(A)+P(B)-P(A and B))

Atomic Concept: Understanding the Fundamental Counting Principle
Key Skills: Use the principle to find the number of possible outcomes

Atomic Concept: Understanding and Calculating Permutations (Order Matters)
Key Skills: Use permutation formula and notation; Solve problems involving ordered arrangements

Atomic Concept: Understanding and Calculating Combinations (Order Does Not Matter)
Key Skills: Use combination formula and notation; Solve problems involving unordered selections

Atomic Concept: Using Counting Principles in Probability Calculations
Key Skills: Apply permutations and combinations to find probabilities of complex events
